text: The surgeon observes a four-centimeter hilar mass with close vascular contact involving two arteries, two veins, and the urinary tract.
The surgeon incises the Gerota's fascia, following the psoas muscle towards the renal hilum, using the DaVinci XI robotic system with four arms.
The surgeon uses a vessel loop to control the posterior branch of the vein and the two arteries, ensuring secure management of these vascular structures during the nephrectomy.
The surgeon manages the adipose tissue to improve visibility in the operating field, ensuring clear access to the anatomical structures involved in the nephrectomy.

The surgeon frees the tumor from the pelvic region, continuing posterior dissection until the anterior branch of the vein is visible. A small artery supplying the tumor is clipped, and the dissection proceeds to separate the tumor from the vein.
The surgeon performs a nuclear resection of the tumor without clamping, and the excised tumor is placed into a retrieval bag.
The surgeon performs hemostasis using a 3-0 monofilament running suture, securing the surgical site to prevent bleeding.
